title: Tab and app focus issues with nightly 1.77.3 1784
author: Clumpton
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
When switching tabs in the app (with mouse) the tab that has the focus needs a mouse click in the window to actually get the focus.
This has changed since the 2019 release. 
Additionally, when MRng is behind another window, clicking in the visible area does not always bring it to the foreground.


## Expected Behavior
Clicking on a connection tab should select the connection and give it the focus.
Clicking somewhere in t...

iterations.description: Focus issue with SSH tabs is confirmed by multiple users on v1.78.2 but lacks reproducible steps, logs, or code pointers to diagnose the root cause.

comments.author: Clumpton
comments.snippet:
> Most likely related to #520

Thanks for that, but the problem is swapping between tabs INSIDE mRemoteNG, not between it and other apps in Windows which is the subject of [#520](https://github.com/mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG/issues/520) .

comments.author: janborup
comments.snippet:
[#520 Option Fix "Allow only a single instance"](https://github.com/mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG/issues/520#issuecomment-355487738)
Thanks this helps for it for me.

comments.author: Mr-Bes
comments.snippet:
The same problem.
When you click to tab with ssh session, you should click to terminal area for get focus and start write commands.
Problem present in v1.78.2.2932
This is the reason why I still use it 1.76.20

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `6c110776` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Tab and app focus issues with nightly 1.77.3 1784

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.
